# Questions in Livestream 163

Live Stream #163: Succeeding at Failed Questions

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=467" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:47</a> | Who are the coastal nations in Genesis 10:5? | He says they are the Mediterranean coastland peoples, especially around the northern Mediterranean, not specifically the Philistines. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=576" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:36</a> | When someone sent a message by Morse code on a telegraph, how did they control who received it? | He says Morse was an open code, so routing depended on operators, call signs, and relay practice rather than secrecy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=930" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:30</a> | Where did the modern idea of the mummy's curse come from? | He says it grew out of British newspaper sensationalism around the Tutankhamun discoveries. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=1112" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:32</a> | Is there a real natural phenomenon behind the east wind in Exodus 14:21? | He says yes and points to the documented wind-setdown phenomenon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=1252" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:52</a> | Why is David's census sinful, and why does one passage mention Satan while another mentions God? | He says the census reflects a deeper trust and power problem, while the texts present divine sovereignty and a proximate adversarial agent together. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=1652" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:32</a> | Was the reported Ramesses II sarcophagus discovery really major news? | He says some finds are real, but the reporting is more sensational than the underlying discovery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=1936" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:16</a> | Will Christian elected officials go to hell if they protect or expand legal abortion? | He treats it as a repeated pastoral question and says persistent deliberate sin reflects being elsewhere in relation to God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=2460" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:00</a> | Where do Abraham and Sarah quote laws to each other? | He says it happens often in Genesis and gives examples rather than an exhaustive list. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=2850" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:30</a> | How should Christians weigh the needs of the many against the needs of the individual? | He rejects the forced either-or and says Scripture makes room for both concerns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=3129" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:09</a> | How do you use a squat toilet? | He gives practical travel advice and warns that Westerners often struggle because they have not developed the habit of squatting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=4721" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:41</a> | In Joshua's long day, where is the requirement that the moon remain in the sky? | He says there is no such requirement and treats the omen framing as neither for nor against Joshua. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=5455" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:55</a> | In the divorce passage, is the indecency current or past? | He says the passage is not clear and could plausibly refer to either. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=5660" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:20</a> | Did a man have to personally write a divorce certificate, and could illiterate men divorce? | He says scribes or hired writers could prepare documents, so literacy was not an impediment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=6146" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:26</a> | Why did God not simply tell Moses to circumcise his son? | He says Moses already knew the covenant obligation, so God was not giving new information. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=6268" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:44:28</a> | Were Midianites descended from Abraham, and how did they relate to Ishmaelites? | He says some were, but Ishmaelites and Midianites are overlapping rather than identical categories. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=6377" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:17</a> | If Moses was circumcised, why did his wife throw the foreskin at his feet? | He says the issue was their son's circumcision and Moses' failure to observe the covenant obligation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=6560" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:20</a> | Could water imagery in biblical narrative point toward living water? | He says that is a good observation and may form a typology leading toward living water. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=6624" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:24</a> | Why is Christianity not henotheistic if Judaism was? | He says Judaism was not henotheistic but monarchal monotheistic, and the categories should not be confused. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=6951" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:51</a> | How does Dr. Falk assess the theory that Isaiah is a Jewish reworking of Greek or Latin literature from Josiah? | He rejects the framing and treats it as an implausible reconstruction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=7434" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:54</a> | How old was David when he fought Goliath? | He says the exact age is not given, but David was clearly young and not yet a full adult warrior. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=7844" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:44</a> | Does Micah 5:2 prove the Messiah is divine or eternal in some way? | He says the passage points in that direction and treats the wording as theologically weighty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=8424" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:24</a> | What is theosis, and how does biblical theosis differ from Mormon theosis? | He says both speak of taking on Godlike attributes, but biblical theosis does not make humans independent gods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=9463" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:43</a> | Did Egyptians count bodies by taking ears or hands? | He says they took right hands, the sword hand, and points to depictions such as Medinet Habu. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=9713" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:53</a> | Does monarchal monotheism imply divinity besides God of a kind? | He says it allows a hierarchy of lesser divine beings while maintaining one supreme God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=10144" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:04</a> | Would an ancient Greek nymph count as a nephesh? | He says yes, that would fit the category. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=10224" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:24</a> | By the Iron Age, how many Israelites were descendants of Jacob rather than assimilated people? | He says that is almost impossible to differentiate because assimilation and intermarriage both occurred. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=10510" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:10</a> | What is the fourth watch? | He initially asks for clarification, then identifies it as the watch of the night in the gospel account of Jesus coming on the water. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=10622" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:02</a> | What are Dr. Falk's thoughts on Judaism originating from Canaanite gods? | He rejects that view and says he sees more traction in a Kenite-related origin hypothesis for Yahweh worship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=10729" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:49</a> | What books or scholarly sources does Dr. Falk recommend on dating the Exodus? | He recommends Kenneth Kitchen, James Hoffmeier, and his own Ark of the Covenant work as starting points. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=12212" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:32</a> | Can Dr. Falk review the Dinesh D'Souza and Alex O'Connor debate and explain where D'Souza went wrong? | He says D'Souza was unprepared and not functioning as a serious apologist in that debate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=12617" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:30:17</a> | Why are experts not invited to serious public discussions like these? | He says many academics will not accept such invitations, and some debate formats prefer punching down rather than expert engagement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=13076" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:56</a> | Is there biblical or extrabiblical evidence that some Black people are related to Israelites in the Bible? | He says generally not, while noting the answer depends on definitions and on the Semitic context of ancient Israel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IKcic5xC9fA?t=13237" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:40:37</a> | How do you know you are being called for ministry? | He says to look for both an internal desire and external recognition or confirmation. |
